provenance_defaults.source: self-authored (general clinical pharmacology knowledge; corroborate against AMH/TGA at review). NOT derived from LITFL notes.

records.mechanism_of_action: inhibits vitamin K epoxide reductase (VKORC1) -> reduced synthesis of clotting factors II, VII, IX, X and proteins C and S
inhibits Na/K-ATPase -> raised intracellular Na then Ca (via Na/Ca exchange); vagotonic autonomic effects
predominantly potassium-channel blockade prolonging repolarisation; also sodium/calcium channel and beta-adrenergic blockade
records.effect: prolonged action potential and refractory period

records.mechanism_of_action: activates AMPK; reduces hepatic gluconeogenesis and increases peripheral insulin sensitivity
agonist at mu-opioid receptors -> reduced neuronal excitability and pain transmission
binds the 30S ribosomal subunit -> irreversible inhibition of bacterial protein synthesis (bactericidal)
selective beta-2 adrenoceptor agonist -> bronchial smooth muscle relaxation

records.effect: reduced heart rate, contractility and AV conduction

records.mechanism_of_action: inhibits the Na-K-2Cl cotransporter in the thick ascending limb of the loop of Henle
irreversible cyclo-oxygenase (COX-1/COX-2) inhibition -> reduced thromboxane A2 (antiplatelet) and prostaglandins
